title: lindsaea lapeyrousei subsp. lapeyrousei
intro: Lindsaea lapeyrousei subsp. lapeyrousei, also known as the Lindsaea fern, is a species of fern in the family Lindsaeaceae, native to New Caledonia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lindsaea lapeyrousei subsp. lapeyrousei include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The fern is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling the plant material in water, and it can also be used as a poultice or applied topically as a paste.
